title: L3 search fast-path + configurable BM25 cap
current_state: tools_memory.py:_hybrid_search_l3 (around lines 332-343) loads up to 1000 L3 entries into memory, tokenizes each, builds a BM25 corpus, runs the BM25 query â€” every call. For small caches (< 200 rows) this is overkill: a simple LIKE + recency + importance score gives equivalent results at a fraction of the CPU and memory cost. For huge caches the LIMIT 1000 is hardcoded; should be tunable per environment.
structural_problem: BM25 is the right tool when corpus size is meaningful. At small scale it is wasted setup cost; at very large scale 1000 may not be enough to find the right results. Both ends benefit from being configurable.
target_state: Two env vars control behaviour: PI_L3_FAST_PATH_THRESHOLD (default 200) and PI_L3_BM25_CAP (default 1000). If row count < threshold, use LIKE-and-score fast path. Otherwise use existing BM25 path but with LIMIT = cap.
files_affected: app/config.py (add the two env vars to the config layer if env-var loading is centralised there; otherwise read directly)
.env.example (add documentation lines for the two vars)
design_notes: Fast-path score formula: score = (1.0 if query.lower() in content.lower() else 0.0) + 0.1 * importance + recency_bonus. Tied to existing scoring style.
recency_bonus = exp(-days_since_created / 7) â€” heavier weight for last week
Row count check: 'SELECT COUNT(*) FROM l3_cache WHERE invalid_at IS NULL' â€” cheap single query
Threshold check happens BEFORE loading any rows â€” only one count query when fast-path triggers
Cap reads from env with int() coercion; on parse error, fall back to default and emit track_silent('config.invalid_env', ValueError(...))
Existing BM25 path stays unchanged except for hardcoded 1000 â†’ cap variable
Fast-path returns the same row shape: (id, content, importance, category, active_until) tuple matching _hybrid_search_l3 contract
success_criteria: When L3 row count < threshold, BM25 setup code is not executed (mock + assert)
Fast-path with 50 rows + query='hello' returns rows in order of LIKE match > importance > recency
BM25 path with 5000 rows uses LIMIT = PI_L3_BM25_CAP (mock the SQL execute call to capture LIMIT arg)
Backward-compat: existing memory tests still PASS without changing env vars
risk_notes: Score formula divergence between fast-path and BM25 path could surface as different result orderings at exactly the threshold boundary. Mitigation: threshold default (200) is conservative; users unlikely to hover at boundary; the two methods agree on high-importance recent rows which dominate ranking.
migration_plan: 1. Read app/config.py to see how env vars are loaded today; add PI_L3_FAST_PATH_THRESHOLD and PI_L3_BM25_CAP if centralised
2. Edit tools_memory.py:_hybrid_search_l3 â€” count query first; if < threshold, fast-path return; else BM25 with capped LIMIT
3. Document defaults and tuning guidance in .env.example
4. Add testing/test_l3_search_fast_path.py: populate 50 rows; query; assert no BM25 import side-effect (or mock check)
5. Add testing/test_l3_search_capped.py: populate 5000 rows; query; assert LIMIT honored
test_plan: test_fast_path_when_under_threshold â€” 50 L3 rows; assert BM25 module not imported (patch import to fail; should still work)
test_bm25_path_when_over_threshold â€” 500 rows; assert BM25 used; LIMIT = cap
test_score_ordering_fast_path â€” query 'hello'; rows with content match score above non-match; high-importance scores above low
test_threshold_env_override â€” set PI_L3_FAST_PATH_THRESHOLD=10; populate 100 rows; assert BM25 path taken
test_cap_env_override â€” set PI_L3_BM25_CAP=100; populate 500; assert LIMIT 100 in query
test_invalid_env_falls_back â€” set PI_L3_BM25_CAP=foo; assert default used; track_silent recorded
